pages.raw_text:
The committee in charge Of the
farmers meeting In support Of the
grand jury Investigations, to be
held at the court house tomorrow,
urge that as many city people as
possible turn out to the meeting.
In order that there may be strong
endorsement Of the work Of the
grand jury.


1t has been arranged for the
meeting to convene in Judge Hin
kle's court room at 10 a. m, and
II this is not sufficient, the Elks
temple will be secured,


c. B. Kegley, president Of the
state grange, and l.. Reed of Span
gle. state organizer Of the Farmers
union, have promised to be present.
Other speakers will be on hand,
and honest government In city.
county and state will be urged. L..
G. Grow Of Palouse, president of
the Farmers union, has been in
vited.


The meeting will be convened by
r. b. Martin, who will state the
object Of its call In talk on county
affairs.
